Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi Bus 101 Timings Capital Park to Dalma Mall Main Entrance

October 8, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Reg Bus 101 is a crucial part of Abu Dhabi’s public transportation network, connecting the bustling city center with the industrial zones. Public transport in Abu Dhabi has seen significant development over the years, and Reg Bus 101 is a testament to the city’s commitment to providing accessible and efficient transportation options.

This bus route not only eases the commute for workers in the industrial areas but also provides convenient access to popular shopping destinations and medical facilities.

Operational Details

Reg Bus 101 operates with frequent services throughout the day, catering to both peak and off-peak hours.

From Capital Park From Dalma Mall Main Entrance
05:55 05:46
06:42 07:14
08:36 07:25
10:39 09:35
12:29 11:29
14:30 13:30
16:40 15:29
18:34 17:23
20:30 19:27
22:26 21:33
00:25 23:10

Download PDF Bus 101

Bus 101 Timetable

To view the full schedule, you can visit the official AD Mobility website or follow this link for 101 Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing and Payment Methods: The fare for Abu Dhabi Bus 101 is AED 2, with an additional AED 0.05 per kilometer. Payment is made using the Hafilat Smart Card, which can be topped up at various locations across the city. The standard fare structure was implemented in February 2024, making it affordable for both residents and visitors. For the most accurate fare information, passengers are advised to check the Abu Dhabi Department of Transport’s official website.

Route Overview

1.

  1. Madinat Zayed Shopping Centre - A bustling shopping destination featuring retail stores, restaurants, and a gold souk.

  2. Mariam Bint Said Mosque - A prominent mosque offering a peaceful place for prayer and reflection.

  3. Abu Dhabi Bus Station - A major hub for local and intercity bus services in Abu Dhabi.

  4. Mohamed Bin Khalifa Street - A key thoroughfare connecting various commercial and residential areas in the city.

  5. Al Jazeera Sports Club - A multipurpose sports complex hosting football matches and other events.

  6. Al Maqta Bus Interchange - A crucial interchange for connecting to other bus routes across the city.

  7. Capital Mall - A shopping mall with a wide range of retail outlets and dining options in the Mussafah area.

  8. Mazyad Mall - A family-friendly shopping destination with stores, entertainment, and dining options.

  9. Bohra Islamic Cultural Complex - A cultural center offering religious services and community activities.

  10. Burjeel Medical City - A state-of-the-art medical facility providing comprehensive healthcare services.

  11. Al Ain University - A leading institution offering higher education and research opportunities.

  12. LLH Medical Centre / Al Najah Private School - A healthcare facility and private school located in the same vicinity.

  13. Mubarak Bin Sumaida Mosque / Al Mussafah Park - A mosque adjacent to a community park, providing both spiritual and recreational spaces.

  14. Al Widad Street - A residential street connecting to various amenities and local services.

16.

  1. Al Daraj Street - A key street in the industrial city, linking various factories and businesses.
Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. First Abu Dhabi Bank - A branch of one of the UAE’s largest banks, offering financial services.

  2. ADNOC - A stop near an ADNOC facility, the UAE’s leading energy company.

  3. Emirates Identity Authority Factory - A stop close to the facility responsible for issuing UAE identity cards.

  4. Dalma Mall West - A part of Dalma Mall offering access to retail stores and dining establishments.

  5. Dalma Mall Main Entrance - The main entry point to one of Abu Dhabi’s largest shopping malls.

The total trip duration is approximately 64 minutes, making it a quick and efficient option for daily travel.

Some other Abu Dhabi bus routes you can check out are the 103 bus route, which runs from Mohamed Bin Zayed City, ADVETI to Capital Park, and the 110 route, which goes from Khalifa Bin Zayed Street to National Petroleum Corporation.

Accessibility Features

Reg Bus 101 is equipped with accessibility features for differently-abled passengers, ensuring a comfortable and convenient journey for everyone. The buses have designated seating areas, ramps, and other necessary accommodations.

Connectivity and Intersections

Reg Bus 101 is well-connected with other bus routes, providing seamless travel across Abu Dhabi. One of the key interchange points is Al Maqta Bus Interchange, where passengers can switch to other bus routes serving different parts of the city. This interchange is vital for commuters traveling to areas not directly served by Reg Bus 101.

This connectivity makes Reg Bus 101 a crucial link for those commuting to work, shopping, or exploring the city.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

Traveling on Reg Bus 101 offers more than just a commute; it provides access to some of Abu Dhabi’s notable attractions and landmarks. Passengers can visit:

  • Al Maqta Fort: A historical landmark near the Al Maqta Bus Interchange.
  • Dalma Mall: A popular destination for shopping, entertainment, and dining.
  • Burjeel Medical City: Ideal for those seeking medical care or visiting patients.

For tourists, this route offers a convenient way to explore various parts of the city, while locals can use it to reach their daily destinations efficiently.

Journey Experience

Starting from Capital Park, nestled in the heart of the city, the bus travels through bustling commercial and residential districts, showcasing modern urban life.

As the route heads toward Abu Dhabi Industrial City, the urban environment transitions into more industrial and suburban zones.

The trip culminates at Dalma Mall, one of Abu Dhabi’s largest shopping and entertainment destinations, providing a convenient end to the journey for those looking to shop or explore.

Tips and Travel Advice

When using Reg Bus 101, it’s best to travel during non-peak hours to avoid crowds. Early morning and late evening trips tend to be less busy. If you’re unfamiliar with the route, consider downloading the Abu Dhabi Department of Transport app for real-time updates and route information.

Reg Bus 101 is an indispensable part of Abu Dhabi’s transportation system, offering reliable and affordable travel options for both daily commuters and tourists. Whether you’re heading to work, shopping, or exploring the city, this route provides a convenient and eco-friendly way to navigate Abu Dhabi.

Embrace the benefits of public transport and contribute to a greener city by choosing Reg Bus 101 for your travel needs. For a complete guide to other routes and services, be sure to check out the Full Abu Dhabi Bus Routes to plan your trips more efficiently.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: October 8, 2025.
